activeCoordinatePolicy: fixed from the first/base Krawczyk active source basis; strict boundary rows are projected into the same active coordinates before entrywise comparison

scaledCompanionIntervalStatus.label: scaled companion interval below Krawczyk capacity
scaledCompanionIntervalStatus.reason: Closed when the safety-inflated refinement interval radius for every scaled companion entry is smaller than the Krawczyk coefficient capacity.

boundaryRowIntervalStatus.label: active boundary-row interval below Krawczyk capacity
boundaryRowIntervalStatus.reason: Closed when the safety-inflated refinement interval radius for every active endpoint boundary-row entry is smaller than the Krawczyk boundary-row capacity.

simultaneousKrawczykInputStatus.label: simultaneous Krawczyk input below half-chart budget
simultaneousKrawczykInputStatus.reason: Closed when both coefficient and boundary intervals fit inside the simultaneous fraction reported by the finite Krawczyk certificate.

refinementTailModelStatus.label: refinement tail model
refinementTailModelStatus.reason: Closed only with tail-mode=geometric when the last two refinement differences support a convergent geometric tail. With tail-mode=none the Krawczyk budget comparison is still valid for the displayed finite refinement radius, but this status remains open as an analytic infinite-tail proof.

finiteKrawczykBudgetStatus.label: finite Krawczyk budget comparison
finiteKrawczykBudgetStatus.reason: Closed when the displayed finite/refinement radii for the fixed-active-coordinate coefficients fit inside both the independent and simultaneous Krawczyk budgets.

exactFiniteKrawczykCoefficientInputStatu.label: exact finite Krawczyk coefficient input
exactFiniteKrawczykCoefficientInputStatu.reason: Closed only after the finite Krawczyk budget comparison and the refinement tail model are both closed.  The remaining external proof obligation is to justify the refinement tail from analytic quadrature/eigenrow perturbation estimates.

fullRankShortcutStatus.label: endpoint full-rank shortcut from coefficient enclosure
fullRankShortcutStatus.reason: If the coefficient intervals are exact, the Krawczyk theorem keeps the endpoint map in the persistent p_01 chart, so the active endpoint map has full row rank and the Green BVP has no active compatibility obstruction.

remainingFormalItem: Replace the refinement/tail model by a theorem bounding the confluent quadrature, r-tail, and eigenrow perturbation errors for every node used in the order-11 collocation system.
